prompt:
Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Natural Language Processing.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: Conditional Random Fields are globally normalized log-linear models that directly parameterize p(y|x) over entire label sequences using transition and state feature functions over labels and arbitrary overlapping input properties, with exact linear-chain inference via forward-backward and Viterbi dynamic programming.
[2] Mech: A neural end-to-end NER architecture composes each token from word embeddings plus character-level representations, contextualizes the sequence with a bidirectional recurrent encoder, and often decodes jointly with a sequence-level label model to enforce valid tag transitions.
[3] Mech: The transformer architecture is an encoder–decoder architecture built entirely from stacked multi-head self-attention mechanism, encoder–decoder attention mechanism, and position-wise feed-forward layers, with positional encodings added to represent token order and masking used for autoregressive decoding.
[4] Mech: The Indra Representation Hypothesis introduces a factorized multimodal latent space in which encoders decompose inputs into modality-agnostic semantic factors and modality-specific surface factors, trained with reconstruction, cross-modal agreement, and topology-preserving alignment regularization over latent units and their relations.
[5] Mech: A family of decoder-only autoregressive transformers is pretrained at scale on expanded public text-and-code corpora and then split into base models and chat models produced by supervised instruction fine-tuning plus human-preference optimization and safety red-teaming.
[6] Mech: Self-Instruct is a self-bootstrapping instruction-tuning pipeline in which a pretrained language model, prompted with a small seed set of tasks, generates new instructions plus optional inputs and outputs, then heuristic filtering, deduplication, and validation select synthetic examples for fine-tuning the same model.
[7] Mech: It uses a unified end-to-end neural network with shared learned word embeddings, multilayer nonlinear feature extraction over local windows or sentence-level contexts, and task-specific structured decoding when label dependencies matter, trained directly from raw or minimally processed text.
[8] Mech: It extends a BiLSTM-CRF sequence tagger with hard parameter sharing across related tagging tasks and a jointly optimized semi-supervised auxiliary objective on unlabeled sequences so that shared lower layers learn general token representations while task-specific output layers handle each label space.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in physics.pop-ph.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: The method separates neutron transport geometry from nuclear reaction processes and uses a neutron balance threshold condition with average path length, scattering distance, and leakage/absorption arguments to derive the critical radius without solving the diffusion equation.
[2] Mech: It models consciousness-relevant physical implementations as effective causal graphs over a finite integration window τ and applies standard black hole causal structure to test whether a single strongly connected component can extend across the horizon.
[3] Mech: The authors factorize the generalized Dirac oscillator into supersymmetric partner Schrödinger-like equations to obtain real spatial eigenvalues and then reconstruct relativistic energies through deformed Magueijo-Smolin and Amelino-Camelia DSR energy maps.
[4] Mech: Using 9 fb−1 of LHCb pp collision data, the study reconstructs χc1(3872) candidates in the J/ψμ+μ− final state and measures the decay rate relative to the reference mode χc1(3872)→J/ψπ+π−.
[5] Mech: It reconstructs Fermi's path by analyzing his early interests in the absolute entropy constant, his engagement with shortcomings of Sommerfeld's quantization for identical particles, and his extension of Pauli's Exclusion Principle to non-interacting particle systems.
[6] Mech: It proposes an analogical synthesis that maps emergence, geometric frustration, competing interactions, spin-glasses, and Mott insulators onto social dynamics and collective-individual relations within a universal consciousness framework.
[7] Mech: The authors combine molecular dynamics simulations of Lennard-Jones and covalently bonded nanoclusters with a continuum-theory treatment of oblique impact that incorporates elasticity, bulk viscosity, and surface tension.
[8] Mech: It uses a Schwinger-Keldysh full-counting-statistics formulation of Krylov position to derive an effective emergent phase-space action for operator growth in the presence of environmental coupling.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in eess.AS.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: EmoShift adds a lightweight EmoSteer layer that learns an emotion-specific steering vector in the output embedding space to shift latent representations toward target emotions.
[2] Mech: It defines the ICASSP 2026 URGENT Challenge through two tracks, curated datasets, baseline systems, evaluation protocols, and comparative result reporting for participating teams.
[3] Mech: The method factorizes each long OverIVA separation filter into a bilinear product of two shorter filters and updates the coupled factors using an alternating iterative projection procedure.
[4] Mech: It introduces a cyclic 2.5D perceptual loss that alternates training supervision across axial, coronal, and sagittal slice views while standardizing PET SUVRs by scanner manufacturer to improve volumetric consistency and preserve clinically relevant uptake patterns.
[5] Mech: WaveSP-Net injects learnable sparse prompts derived from partial wavelet-domain multi-resolution features into a frozen XLSR front-end and pairs it with a bidirectional Mamba back-end for artifact localization and classification.
[6] Mech: CryoLithe uses an end-to-end, local, memory-efficient deep network that reconstructs volumes directly from aligned tilt series by exploiting transform-domain locality instead of post-processing an FBP initialization.
[7] Mech: It formulates HDR modulo recovery as an optimization problem with a deep prior and unrolls the solver into a lightweight optimization-inspired neural network with a convolutional denoiser and a scaling-equivariance-based self-supervised fine-tuning term.
[8] Mech: DIVINE uses embeddings from pretrained audio and video foundation models and explicitly disentangles shared and modality-specific factors via hierarchical variational bottlenecks, then combines them with sparse gated fusion and learnable symptom tokens in a multitask classifier.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in physics.plasm-ph.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: It analyzes the infinite-permeability limit of the Laplace boundary-value problem to show that the exterior solution is fixed entirely by the body's outer boundary geometry, regardless of interior details.
[2] Mech: It characterizes AXUV-HS5 photodiode nonlinear response with pulsed-laser calibration and then estimates plasma density, size, temperature, and duration by combining charge-conserving soft x-ray measurements with a spherical emission model and the Bennett relation.
[3] Mech: It constructs a symmetry-representation-based real-space Hamiltonian that treats the electric field as a vector in near and far fields, can deform between tight-binding and long-range photonic limits, and independently tunes symmetry-resolved mode energies at high-symmetry Brillouin-zone points.
[4] Mech: It analyzes the generalized magnetic field \(\vec{\mathcal{B}} = \vec{B} + \nabla\times((c/\omega_{pe})^2\mu_0\vec{j})\) and proves in 3D that this field is Lie-dragged by an evolution velocity even when ordinary magnetic field lines reconnect.
[5] Mech: It combines quantum Monte Carlo calculations with many-body ab initio interaction potentials that explicitly represent the proton coordination environment across cluster sizes.
[6] Mech: The authors develop an analytical and numerical formalism that folds Doppler-broadened XFEL spectra into SAXS coherence calculations and apply it to benchmark single-step and periodic-grating geometries to estimate intensity reduction, Bragg-peak suppression, and peak broadening.
[7] Mech: The authors analyze the classical motion of an open string coupled to a constant electric field and map it via the double-copy prescription to a closed string propagating in the corresponding double-copied spacetime background, then compare the resulting dynamics and pathologies.
[8] Mech: The paper develops a time-domain Method of Auxiliary Sources coupled with a first-order Standard Impedance Boundary Condition to model scattering through surface fields only, avoiding computation of internal fields.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in math.GR.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: It derives sufficient geometric conditions by comparing corresponding sections or projections of the nested pair and by analyzing support cones or point-source shadow boundaries with apexes on the boundary of K.
[2] Mech: The paper develops inverse scattering for these nonzero step-like boundary conditions, formulates the solution as a Riemann-Hilbert problem, and applies nonlinear steepest descent to extract asymptotics in distinct space-time regions.
[3] Mech: It introduces a new formal language of universes, induced communities, cells, and spots and uses this framework to derive a proof of the conjecture.
[4] Mech: It derives similarity variables and an analytical local self-similar solution by reducing general strictly hyperbolic dynamics near shock onset to Burgers-like behavior.
[5] Mech: It proves structural results by analyzing spherical conjugacy classes and growth in abelian-by-cyclic semidirect products, then contrasts these with an explicit one-sided Følner sequence construction in BS(1,2).
[6] Mech: It combines variational analysis via optimizers of an interpolation Hardy-Gagliardo-Nirenberg inequality, Pohozaev identity arguments, and a compactness method to derive ground states, nonexistence results, global/blow-up criteria, and minimal-mass blow-up characterization.
[7] Mech: It defines this new operator class through measure-induced matrix entries and derives boundedness criteria by analyzing the interaction between the measure and the underlying weighted sequence norms.
[8] Mech: It uses formal-language and group-theoretic arguments to prove non-EDT0Lness for the infinite cyclic group's word problem and to derive invariance under changing generators and passing to finitely generated subgroups.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Database Systems.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: C-Store is a column-oriented DBMS that stores attributes separately in sorted compressed projections, uses a write-optimized update store merged into a read-optimized main store, and executes queries with late materialization over replicated and partitioned projections.
[2] Mech: MonetDB/X100 uses a vectorized, column-oriented execution engine in which relational operators run as tight kernels over small batches of typed column values, exchanging vectors between operators to balance pipelining with limited materialization and late tuple reconstruction.
[3] Mech: It uses a controlled architectural decomposition study that incrementally adds column-store techniques—vertical partitioning, late materialization, block/vectorized processing, aggressive compression, and lightweight positional metadata for tuple reassembly—into a row-store to isolate each mechanism’s effect.
[4] Mech: It introduces learned index structures, especially a recursive model index that trains hierarchical models to approximate the key-to-position mapping given by the data CDF and then uses tracked error bounds with bounded local search to recover exact results.
[5] Mech: The method uses a learned piecewise key-to-rank predictor augmented with update-aware metadata and local correction structures that track positional shifts, so prediction is separated from efficient exact rank recovery after updates.
[6] Mech: It introduces a materialization-aware columnar execution strategy that keeps data in separate aligned columns with lightweight tuple identifiers through selections, joins, projections, and aggregations, and chooses among early, late, and hybrid tuple reconstruction points based on operator needs and cost tradeoffs.
[7] Mech: RadixSpline builds in a single sequential pass a bounded-error piecewise linear spline over sorted keys and a radix directory from key prefixes to spline segments, so a lookup predicts position with the spline and then performs a short bounded local search.
[8] Mech: ALEX is a hierarchical learned index that uses lightweight regression models to route keys to leaf nodes, where sorted arrays with reserved gaps absorb inserts locally, while cost models trigger local expansion, retraining, or splitting based on access and update behavior.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Database Systems.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: BwTree is a lock-free B-tree variant that replaces in-place page updates with append-only delta records and uses an atomically updated mapping table to decouple each logical node from its physical storage location while preserving ordered traversal via side links.
[2] Mech: The Log-Structured Merge-Tree (LSM-Tree) buffers updates in an in-memory sorted component and periodically merges them into a hierarchy of larger on-disk sorted components, reconciling newer and older entries in batch to turn random updates into sequential writes.
[3] Mech: It proposes a one-pass streaming wavelet synopsis that incrementally updates and retains only the most significant hierarchical wavelet coefficients of the evolving frequency signal to support approximate reconstruction and aggregate estimation without materializing the full data.
[4] Mech: The method constructs a congressional sample, a composite synopsis that combines a population-proportional sample with a group-coverage-oriented component and answers group-by aggregates by appropriately weighting sampled tuples.
[5] Mech: It introduces learned index structures, especially a recursive model index that trains hierarchical models to approximate the key-to-position mapping given by the data CDF and then uses tracked error bounds with bounded local search to recover exact results.
[6] Mech: Adaptive Radix Tree (ART) indexes keys bytewise in a radix trie whose internal nodes dynamically switch among multiple node layouts of different capacities, combined with path compression and lazy expansion to keep the structure compact and fast.
[7] Mech: It introduces random sampling as a first-class relational operation, defining tuple-selection procedures with characterized inclusion probabilities under database storage constraints, including sequential access, limited memory, and both with- and without-replacement sampling, while analyzing interaction with relational operators.
[8] Mech: BlinkDB is a distributed approximate query processing system that precomputes a family of workload-aware multi-dimensional stratified samples and, at query time, uses an optimizer to choose the smallest sample predicted to meet a user-specified latency or accuracy bound while returning aggregates with confidence estimates.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Machine Learning.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: Constitutional AI trains an assistant with a written constitution by having the model self-critique and revise its answers under those rules for supervised finetuning, then generate constitution-based preference comparisons to train a preference model and optimize the assistant with reinforcement learning.
[2] Mech: Model-R uses residual blocks that learn a residual function F(x) and add it elementwise to an identity or projected shortcut x, with bottleneck blocks for very deep models and global average pooling before the classifier.
[3] Mech: Instruction tuning finetunes a large pretrained sequence-to-sequence model on a diverse mixture of heterogeneous NLP datasets reformatted into a unified text-to-text instruction/output format so the model learns to map natural-language instructions directly to task behavior.
[4] Mech: VGG uses a homogeneous deep convolutional architecture that stacks many small 3x3 convolutions, with occasional 1x1 convolutions and sparse pooling, followed by fully connected classification layers.
[5] Mech: The method combines supervised fine-tuning on human-written instruction demonstrations with reinforcement learning from a learned reward model trained on pairwise human preference rankings, using a KL penalty to keep the policy close to the supervised model during policy optimization.
[6] Mech: The method uses Histograms of Oriented Gradients (HOG), computing dense local gradient orientation histograms over small cells, normalizing them within overlapping blocks, and classifying fixed-size sliding windows across position and scale with a linear margin-based classifier.
[7] Mech: GPT-3-style autoregressive language model is a massively scaled decoder-only autoregressive transformer architecture trained on next-token prediction over a huge diverse text corpus and evaluated by placing natural-language instructions plus zero, one, or a few input-output examples directly in the context window without updating parameters.
[8] Mech: A deep supervised convolutional neural network is trained end-to-end on GPUs using stacked convolution and overlapping pooling layers, ReLU activations, fully connected classifiers, dropout, data augmentation, and model parallelism.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in stat.CO.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: It proposes a simulation-based imputation procedure that combines Markov chain transition modeling with random-walk principles to fill in unobserved fish positions over time.
[2] Mech: It develops a pairing theory in which electrons in the KTaO3 2DEG interact via exchange of the soft transverse optic Slater mode characteristic of a quantum paraelectric and evaluates the resulting superconducting coupling and gap anisotropy for different interface orientations.
[3] Mech: The paper implements mixture-of-experts distributional regression in an R package using TensorFlow-based stochastic first-order optimization with adaptive learning-rate schedules and robust estimation procedures across multiple response families.
[4] Mech: It uses a Bayesian latent class joint model for primary and key secondary endpoints with risk-factor adjustment to generate individualized dynamic k-step-ahead predictions and flag conflicting or anomalous future observations.
[5] Mech: The paper computes second-order reliability method estimates by finding the most probable path and numerically evaluating the associated infinite-dimensional fluctuation corrections, including operator traces and Carleman-Fredholm determinants, using scalable discretization-aware algorithms implemented with automatic differentiation in JAX.
[6] Mech: The paper develops a composite-likelihood EM procedure that uses importance-sampling estimates, alongside a Monte Carlo EM baseline, to approximate maximum-likelihood inference without full high-dimensional integration.
[7] Mech: The paper uses optically excited gold-silica Janus nanoparticles whose asymmetric photothermal heating generates self-thermophoretic propulsion, quantified by single-particle tracking.
[8] Mech: It uses atomistic simulations of a generic uncharged linear polymer in water with non-specific van der Waals polymer-water and polymer-ion interactions while varying NaSCN or NaI concentration at fixed Na2SO4 background to analyze ion accumulation, depletion, and polymer conformational changes.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Database Systems.

[1] Mech: It introduces learned index structures, especially a recursive model index that trains hierarchical models to approximate the key-to-position mapping given by the data CDF and then uses tracked error bounds with bounded local search to recover exact results.
[2] Mech: Paxos uses proposers, acceptors, and learners in a two-phase protocol with monotonically increasing proposal numbers, where prepare requests establish an ordering and accept requests are constrained to preserve any value that may already have been chosen.
[3] Mech: It introduces an R* distributed transaction management architecture in which a global transaction coordinator drives two-phase commit and distributed concurrency control across sites while local resource managers independently handle execution, locking, logging, and recovery for subordinate actions.
[4] Mech: Paxos uses numbered proposals and a two-phase quorum protocol in which proposers first obtain promises from a majority of acceptors not to accept lower-numbered proposals, then submit a value constrained by the highest-numbered prior acceptance reported, with learners determining the chosen value from quorum intersections.
[5] Mech: RadixSpline builds in a single sequential pass a bounded-error piecewise linear spline over sorted keys and a radix directory from key prefixes to spline segments, so a lookup predicts position with the spline and then performs a short bounded local search.
[6] Mech: It implements a production Paxos-based replication system that separates the core safety protocol from engineering subsystems such as persistent logging, leader failover, replica recovery and catch-up, membership handling, checksums, retransmission/timeout control, monitoring, and service interfaces around an ordered durable log.
[7] Mech: ALEX is a hierarchical learned index that uses lightweight regression models to route keys to leaf nodes, where sorted arrays with reserved gaps absorb inserts locally, while cost models trigger local expansion, retraining, or splitting based on access and update behavior.
[8] Mech: It introduces a seven-dimensional analytical taxonomy that decomposes hashing methods by hash behavior, bucket organization, collision handling, storage layout, workload sensitivity, update support, and query semantics, then uses this framework to map index design choices to query-processing requirements.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Computer Vision.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: StyleGAN replaces direct latent input to the generator with a learned mapping network that produces an intermediate latent code whose per-layer style parameters modulate each convolution via adaptive normalization, while independent noise is injected at each layer and style mixing is used during training.
[2] Mech: It uses an end-to-end trained multilayer convolutional neural network with local receptive fields, shared weights, alternating learned filtering and subsampling stages, and backpropagation to jointly optimize feature extraction and classification from raw pixels.
[3] Mech: A deep supervised convolutional neural network is trained end-to-end on GPUs using stacked convolution and overlapping pooling layers, ReLU activations, fully connected classifiers, dropout, data augmentation, and model parallelism.
[4] Mech: VGG uses a homogeneous deep convolutional architecture that stacks many small 3x3 convolutions, with occasional 1x1 convolutions and sparse pooling, followed by fully connected classification layers.
[5] Mech: DCGAN uses a fully convolutional Model-GAN in which the generator maps latent vectors to images with stacked fractional-stride convolutions and the discriminator classifies images with strided convolutions, combined with architectural constraints such as removing pooling and most fully connected layers, extensive batch normalization, and asymmetric activations to stabilize training.
[6] Mech: Dataset-I constructs a hierarchical image dataset by mapping object categories to the WordNet lexical taxonomy, automatically harvesting candidate web images via query expansion and search, and then using distributed human verification to filter and label them at scale.
[7] Mech: Progressive Growing of GANs trains the generator and discriminator by starting at very low image resolution and incrementally adding higher-resolution layers with smooth fade-in transitions, alongside signal-control tricks and a minibatch standard-deviation feature in the discriminator.
[8] Mech: StyleGAN2 replaces progressive growing and heuristic resolution changes with a fixed-resolution style-based generator that uses explicitly low-pass-filtered upsampling/downsampling and revised normalization/noise injection to preserve signal alignment across scales.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Computer Vision.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models define a fixed Gaussian forward Markov chain that gradually corrupts data and train a timestep-conditioned neural network to parameterize the reverse chain by predicting the added noise under a simplified variational objective.
[2] Mech: DCGAN uses a fully convolutional Model-GAN in which the generator maps latent vectors to images with stacked fractional-stride convolutions and the discriminator classifies images with strided convolutions, combined with architectural constraints such as removing pooling and most fully connected layers, extensive batch normalization, and asymmetric activations to stabilize training.
[3] Mech: Generative Adversarial Nets train a generator that maps latent noise to synthetic data and a discriminator that predicts real versus generated inputs in a simultaneous differentiable minimax game, with the generator updated through gradients propagated across the discriminator.
[4] Mech: StyleGAN replaces direct latent input to the generator with a learned mapping network that produces an intermediate latent code whose per-layer style parameters modulate each convolution via adaptive normalization, while independent noise is injected at each layer and style mixing is used during training.
[5] Mech: Progressive Growing of GANs trains the generator and discriminator by starting at very low image resolution and incrementally adding higher-resolution layers with smooth fade-in transitions, alongside signal-control tricks and a minibatch standard-deviation feature in the discriminator.
[6] Mech: Generative Adversarial Networks train a generator that maps simple random noise to data samples and a discriminator that classifies real versus generated inputs, with both optimized jointly through a minimax adversarial objective so the generator implicitly matches the data distribution without explicit likelihoods.
[7] Mech: LAPGAN uses a Laplacian pyramid of separately trained GANs, where a base generator samples a low-resolution image from noise and each higher conditional Model-GAN predicts the residual details needed to refine the upsampled image at that scale.
[8] Mech: Conditional Model-GAN augments the standard Model-GAN minimax game by feeding the same conditioning variable y into both the generator, which maps noise and y to samples, and the discriminator, which judges whether a sample-y pair is real or generated.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in stat.ML.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: The paper formulates a Laplacian-constrained GGM that jointly incorporates node signal data and text-derived metadata and solves the resulting optimization with a majorization-minimization algorithm using closed-form updates.
[2] Mech: The paper formulates streaming inference as a forward-KL projection in predictive-distribution space and derives a fully recursive deterministic beam-search algorithm that keeps the renormalized top-S posterior-weighted regime paths with closed-form predictive updates.
[3] Mech: It introduces P-tensors as a general mathematical formalism for defining higher-order permutation-equivariant message passing over subgraphs and related structures within graph neural networks.
[4] Mech: It decomposes calibration variance with a frequentist component analysis over 292 million trades and validates the structure with a Bayesian hierarchical model.
[5] Mech: It applies Singular Learning Theory and the local learning coefficient to characterize competing near-zero-loss basins and track training dynamics as a phase transition between them.
[6] Mech: The paper uses a Gaussian process surrogate and acquisition functions derived from the joint posterior of GP gradients to sequentially select new evaluations that improve Sobol' index and DGSM estimation.
[7] Mech: The paper introduces two model-free Q-learning algorithms, Q-EarlySettled-LowCost and FedQ-EarlySettled-LowCost, designed to settle policies early while maintaining low switching or communication frequency and near-optimal regret.
[8] Mech: The paper builds a tensor from daily precipitation, maximum temperature, and minimum temperature data and uses a spatio-temporal principal component analysis to initialize CP decomposition while explicitly incorporating spatial and temporal structure.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in q-bio.BM.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: VCWorld builds a white-box biological world model by combining structured biological knowledge with the iterative reasoning of large language models to simulate perturbation-driven signaling cascades step by step and generate mechanistic hypotheses.
[2] Mech: FLOWR.root is an SE(3)-equivariant flow-matching foundation model jointly trained for structure-aware 3D ligand generation, confidence-aware affinity prediction, conditional sampling, and parameter-efficient LoRA domain adaptation on protein-ligand data of mixed fidelity.
[3] Mech: It synthesizes and organizes recent computational approaches for single-cell GRN inference across modalities by comparing how they model regulatory interactions, dynamic state changes, and perturbation responses.
[4] Mech: It combines a generative procedure for synthetic functional landscapes with a graph neural network that explicitly represents domain connectivity and then uses transfer learning to adapt to biological datasets.
[5] Mech: It proposes a first-principles HSF posterior sourcing framework that classifies fossil molecular environments by origin, deamination, and similarity indicators, treats fragments as primary units, maximizes source diversity, defers lineage assignment, and uses phylogenetic consistency plus preservation characterization to trace fragment origins.
[6] Mech: The authors use Q-learning agents in repeated two-person Prisoner's Dilemma games and compare cooperative dynamics across three information perception scenarios, including asymmetric information.
[7] Mech: It builds an OWL ontology in Protege, augments it with SWRL rules for rule-based inference, and evaluates reasoning using SPARQL queries over six sample patient scenarios.
[8] Mech: The paper uses a commentary-style overview to synthesize recent developments in generative AI and synthetic data and map their potential uses and risks for infectious disease research.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Machine Learning.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models define a fixed Gaussian forward Markov chain that gradually corrupts data and train a timestep-conditioned neural network to parameterize the reverse chain by predicting the added noise under a simplified variational objective.
[2] Mech: Generative Adversarial Nets train a generator that maps latent noise to synthetic data and a discriminator that predicts real versus generated inputs in a simultaneous differentiable minimax game, with the generator updated through gradients propagated across the discriminator.
[3] Mech: Diffusion probabilistic modeling defines a fixed forward Markov chain that gradually adds small Gaussian noise to data until it reaches a simple tractable distribution, then learns a parameterized reverse Markov chain with shared parameters by optimizing a variational log-likelihood bound to invert each noising step.
[4] Mech: It uses a denoising diffusion probabilistic model in which a U-Net-like network with attention mechanism is trained across many timesteps to predict added Gaussian noise under a carefully designed variance schedule, then samples by iteratively reversing the fixed noising process from pure noise.
[5] Mech: It introduces score-based generative modeling through stochastic differential equations, learning a time-conditioned score function over a continuous forward noising process and generating data by simulating the corresponding reverse-time SDE or its probability-flow ODE, with predictor-corrector refinement.
[6] Mech: DCGAN uses a fully convolutional Model-GAN in which the generator maps latent vectors to images with stacked fractional-stride convolutions and the discriminator classifies images with strided convolutions, combined with architectural constraints such as removing pooling and most fully connected layers, extensive batch normalization, and asymmetric activations to stabilize training.
[7] Mech: Progressive Growing of GANs trains the generator and discriminator by starting at very low image resolution and incrementally adding higher-resolution layers with smooth fade-in transitions, alongside signal-control tricks and a minibatch standard-deviation feature in the discriminator.
[8] Mech: Conditional Model-GAN augments the standard Model-GAN minimax game by feeding the same conditioning variable y into both the generator, which maps noise and y to samples, and the discriminator, which judges whether a sample-y pair is real or generated.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Robotics.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: Series Elastic Actuation inserts a deliberately compliant spring in series between the motor/transmission and the load, measures spring deflection to infer output force via Hooke’s law, and closes the controller around force tracking rather than pure position tracking.
[2] Mech: Hybrid zero-dynamics embedding: the method imposes feedback-enforced virtual constraints on an asymmetric hopper and constructs an impact-invariant reduced manifold so the closed-loop hybrid system evolves exactly as a Spring Loaded Inverted Pendulum template.
[3] Mech: It introduces a hybrid feedback control framework that stabilizes periodic bipedal gaits by continuously regulating reduced-order gait variables and virtual joint constraints during swing and stance, while applying event-based corrections at foot impact and analyzing step-to-step stability with return maps.
[4] Mech: A balance-centered feedback control method uses simplified whole-body dynamics to regulate center-of-mass motion, body orientation, and ground reaction/contact forces across hybrid support phases instead of tracking fully precomputed gait trajectories.
[5] Mech: A monolithic multigait soft robot uses elastomeric body segments with embedded fluidic channel networks that are selectively pressurized in different temporal patterns to produce distributed bending, anchoring, and release sequences for locomotion.
[6] Mech: An untethered soft pneumatic robot architecture integrates onboard power and pressure generation with embedded fluidic control and co-designed limb/body geometry so cyclic internal pressurization produces a coordinated crawling gait.
[7] Mech: Soft robotics paradigm: replace rigid-link, jointed, transmission-centric robot architectures with elastomeric or continuum bodies, distributed actuation, and morphology-driven control in which passive body mechanics and environmental contact perform part of sensing, adaptation, and control.
[8] Mech: Passive dynamic walking models a two-link biped descending a shallow slope as a hybrid dynamical system with continuous gravity-driven swing/stance motion and discrete heel-strike collision maps, then analyzes periodic step cycles and their stability from the mechanism's physical parameters alone.

[1] Mech: It decomposes calibration variance with a frequentist component analysis over 292 million trades and validates the structure with a Bayesian hierarchical model.
[2] Mech: It introduces P-tensors as a general mathematical formalism for defining higher-order permutation-equivariant message passing over subgraphs and related structures within graph neural networks.
[3] Mech: The paper uses a Gaussian process surrogate and acquisition functions derived from the joint posterior of GP gradients to sequentially select new evaluations that improve Sobol' index and DGSM estimation.
[4] Mech: The paper introduces two model-free Q-learning algorithms, Q-EarlySettled-LowCost and FedQ-EarlySettled-LowCost, designed to settle policies early while maintaining low switching or communication frequency and near-optimal regret.
[5] Mech: The paper builds a tensor from daily precipitation, maximum temperature, and minimum temperature data and uses a spatio-temporal principal component analysis to initialize CP decomposition while explicitly incorporating spatial and temporal structure.
[6] Mech: The paper formulates streaming inference as a forward-KL projection in predictive-distribution space and derives a fully recursive deterministic beam-search algorithm that keeps the renormalized top-S posterior-weighted regime paths with closed-form predictive updates.
[7] Mech: The paper formulates a Laplacian-constrained GGM that jointly incorporates node signal data and text-derived metadata and solves the resulting optimization with a majorization-minimization algorithm using closed-form updates.
[8] Mech: It applies Singular Learning Theory and the local learning coefficient to characterize competing near-zero-loss basins and track training dynamics as a phase transition between them.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Machine Learning.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: Constitutional AI trains an assistant with a written constitution by having the model self-critique and revise its answers under those rules for supervised finetuning, then generate constitution-based preference comparisons to train a preference model and optimize the assistant with reinforcement learning.
[2] Mech: Stochastic Neighbor Embedding models each point's neighbors in the high-dimensional space as a Gaussian-based conditional probability distribution and learns low-dimensional coordinates by gradient descent to minimize the divergence between these high- and low-dimensional neighborhood distributions.
[3] Mech: The method combines supervised fine-tuning on human-written instruction demonstrations with reinforcement learning from a learned reward model trained on pairwise human preference rankings, using a KL penalty to keep the policy close to the supervised model during policy optimization.
[4] Mech: GPT-3-style autoregressive language model is a massively scaled decoder-only autoregressive transformer architecture trained on next-token prediction over a huge diverse text corpus and evaluated by placing natural-language instructions plus zero, one, or a few input-output examples directly in the context window without updating parameters.
[5] Mech: t-SNE converts high-dimensional pairwise distances into adaptive local similarity probabilities, defines low-dimensional similarities with a Student-t distribution with one degree of freedom, and optimizes a symmetrized KL-divergence objective by gradient descent to balance attraction and repulsion in the embedding.
[6] Mech: Isomap constructs a neighborhood graph among nearby samples, approximates manifold geodesic distances by shortest-path distances on that graph, and then applies classical multidimensional scaling to those distances to recover low-dimensional coordinates.
[7] Mech: Locally Linear Embedding constructs a nearest-neighbor graph, computes for each point constrained linear reconstruction weights from its neighbors, and then finds low-dimensional coordinates via a sparse eigenproblem that preserve those same weights in the embedding.
[8] Mech: Instruction tuning finetunes a large pretrained sequence-to-sequence model on a diverse mixture of heterogeneous NLP datasets reformatted into a unified text-to-text instruction/output format so the model learns to map natural-language instructions directly to task behavior.

[1] Mech: The paper applies Bayesian optimization over five TFT pulse-operation parameters using normalized degree of separation as the objective, then validates the resulting settings on 4-bit and 6-bit temporal encoding and image-sequence reconstruction, with SHAP used for parameter-importance analysis.
[2] Mech: It defines several aura-specific compactness and connectedness notions, constructs subspace and product aura topologies, and proves implication, preservation, and equivalence theorems under conditions such as transitivity and aura separation axioms.
[3] Mech: It defines res-constructible extensions via definable closure from a tuple in the valuation ring whose residues are dcl-independent over the base residue field, and then analyzes intermediate models through residue-field and value-group structure.
[4] Mech: The paper builds a terahertz time-domain spectroscopy setup integrated with a Bluefors dilution refrigerator and a 7 T split-coil magnet, then measures terahertz waveforms from a GaAs single quantum well between 0 and 6 T at 145 mK.
[5] Mech: It proposes PLANCK, a physics-inspired deep reinforcement learning solver built on hypergraph neural networks that directly models arbitrary high-order interactions and exploits gauge symmetry during training and inference.
[6] Mech: It develops constructive and structural arguments in metric geometry and topological group settings to show non-plasticity for countable dense subsets of normed spaces and to build plastic dense subspaces or subgroups inside broader separable spaces and groups.
[7] Mech: It constructs linear orders from either refining chains witnessing chainability or inverse-limit representations by arcs, each combined with a fixed non-principal ultrafilter on the natural numbers, and then analyzes their order-theoretic and descriptive-set-theoretic properties.
[8] Mech: It formulates fixed point theorems under the perturbed metric space framework for F-perturbed mappings, proves them analytically, tests necessity through a counterexample, and then uses the theorem to derive existence of a boundary value problem solution.

[1] Mech: The paper develops inverse scattering for these nonzero step-like boundary conditions, formulates the solution as a Riemann-Hilbert problem, and applies nonlinear steepest descent to extract asymptotics in distinct space-time regions.

[4] Mech: It combines variational analysis via optimizers of an interpolation Hardy-Gagliardo-Nirenberg inequality, Pohozaev identity arguments, and a compactness method to derive ground states, nonexistence results, global/blow-up criteria, and minimal-mass blow-up characterization.

[6] Mech: It introduces a new formal language of universes, induced communities, cells, and spots and uses this framework to derive a proof of the conjecture.
[7] Mech: It defines this new operator class through measure-induced matrix entries and derives boundedness criteria by analyzing the interaction between the measure and the underlying weighted sequence norms.
[8] Mech: It derives sufficient geometric conditions by comparing corresponding sections or projections of the nested pair and by analyzing support cones or point-source shadow boundaries with apexes on the boundary of K.

[1] Mech: The authors develop and compare three recovery algorithms for SPHINCS_BSSN with tabulated EOSs: a 3D Newton-Raphson solver, a 2D Newton-Raphson solver, and a 1D Ridders-based root-finding method used as a robust fallback.
[2] Mech: It uses multiple-lens-plane simulations with Monte Carlo generated cluster light cones modeled by truncated NFW halos, combined with semi-numerical and EOS source light cones, and measures lensed versus unlensed bubble size distributions via the Mean Free Path method.
[3] Mech: It combines extended Phase-II GLEAM-X observations with earlier GLEAM survey data to produce 72-231 MHz multi-band images sensitive to angular scales from 45 arcseconds to 15 degrees and performs source-finding on a 170-231 MHz wide-band image.
[4] Mech: It extends SMICA beyond second-order statistics by incorporating bispectrum information and introduces a binned multi-frequency bispectrum estimator that jointly infers multiple components directly from channel maps.
[5] Mech: The authors compute the two-point correlation function of transmitted flux in the CIV forest of the UVES spectrum of HE0940-1050, deabsorb strong CGM-associated systems, and compare the measured signal against 1000 shuffled-line mock spectra and additional synthetic-noise tests.
[6] Mech: The authors generate mock observations of a simulated barred galaxy to measure orientation-dependent changes in mass-weighted λ_R and then compare weakly and strongly barred galaxies in the SAMI survey using stellar age, λ_R, and specific star formation rate within one effective radius.
[7] Mech: It analyzes the effect of neutrino flavor conversion inside the proto-neutron star on convection and predicts the resulting changes in gravitational-wave emission amplitude and frequency content during the post-bounce phase.
[8] Mech: It takes CompOSE tabulated equations of state with a first-order hadron-quark transition, smooths them via a percolation construction into two second- or third-order transitions, and compares merger simulations across binaries matched in single-star mass, radius, and tidal deformability.

[1] Mech: It constructs a symmetry-representation-based real-space Hamiltonian that treats the electric field as a vector in near and far fields, can deform between tight-binding and long-range photonic limits, and independently tunes symmetry-resolved mode energies at high-symmetry Brillouin-zone points.
[2] Mech: The paper develops a time-domain Method of Auxiliary Sources coupled with a first-order Standard Impedance Boundary Condition to model scattering through surface fields only, avoiding computation of internal fields.
[3] Mech: It analyzes the infinite-permeability limit of the Laplace boundary-value problem to show that the exterior solution is fixed entirely by the body's outer boundary geometry, regardless of interior details.
[4] Mech: The authors analyze the classical motion of an open string coupled to a constant electric field and map it via the double-copy prescription to a closed string propagating in the corresponding double-copied spacetime background, then compare the resulting dynamics and pathologies.
[5] Mech: The authors develop an analytical and numerical formalism that folds Doppler-broadened XFEL spectra into SAXS coherence calculations and apply it to benchmark single-step and periodic-grating geometries to estimate intensity reduction, Bragg-peak suppression, and peak broadening.
[6] Mech: It characterizes AXUV-HS5 photodiode nonlinear response with pulsed-laser calibration and then estimates plasma density, size, temperature, and duration by combining charge-conserving soft x-ray measurements with a spherical emission model and the Bennett relation.
[7] Mech: It analyzes the generalized magnetic field \(\vec{\mathcal{B}} = \vec{B} + \nabla\times((c/\omega_{pe})^2\mu_0\vec{j})\) and proves in 3D that this field is Lie-dragged by an evolution velocity even when ordinary magnetic field lines reconnect.
[8] Mech: It combines quantum Monte Carlo calculations with many-body ab initio interaction potentials that explicitly represent the proton coordination environment across cluster sizes.

[1] Mech: DIVINE uses embeddings from pretrained audio and video foundation models and explicitly disentangles shared and modality-specific factors via hierarchical variational bottlenecks, then combines them with sparse gated fusion and learnable symptom tokens in a multitask classifier.
[2] Mech: WaveSP-Net injects learnable sparse prompts derived from partial wavelet-domain multi-resolution features into a frozen XLSR front-end and pairs it with a bidirectional Mamba back-end for artifact localization and classification.
[3] Mech: It formulates HDR modulo recovery as an optimization problem with a deep prior and unrolls the solver into a lightweight optimization-inspired neural network with a convolutional denoiser and a scaling-equivariance-based self-supervised fine-tuning term.
[4] Mech: EmoShift adds a lightweight EmoSteer layer that learns an emotion-specific steering vector in the output embedding space to shift latent representations toward target emotions.
[5] Mech: The method factorizes each long OverIVA separation filter into a bilinear product of two shorter filters and updates the coupled factors using an alternating iterative projection procedure.
[6] Mech: CryoLithe uses an end-to-end, local, memory-efficient deep network that reconstructs volumes directly from aligned tilt series by exploiting transform-domain locality instead of post-processing an FBP initialization.
[7] Mech: It introduces a cyclic 2.5D perceptual loss that alternates training supervision across axial, coronal, and sagittal slice views while standardizing PET SUVRs by scanner manufacturer to improve volumetric consistency and preserve clinically relevant uptake patterns.
[8] Mech: It defines the ICASSP 2026 URGENT Challenge through two tracks, curated datasets, baseline systems, evaluation protocols, and comparative result reporting for participating teams.

Below are 8 genomes from 2 different research lineages (4 each) in Natural Language Processing.
They are shuffled. Identify which 4 belong to Group A and which 4 to Group B.
ALSO order each group chronologically (earliest → latest).

[1] Mech: It proposes a theory-driven NLIDB framework that decomposes processing into user-intent representation, schema-level semantic interpretation, and translation into executable formal database queries, mediated by constrained intermediate semantic forms.
[2] Mech: It trains a single shared multilingual encoder over monolingual corpora with a joint subword vocabulary, combining masked/denoising language modeling and cross-lingual reconstruction objectives to induce a common latent space across languages.
[3] Mech: A reusable QA test collection construction procedure adapts classical IR test-collection methodology by assembling natural-language questions, a large corpus, and human relevance judgments that mark answer-bearing documents or snippets while accounting for paraphrase, ambiguity, and multiple valid answer forms.
[4] Mech: Multilingual Model-B pretrains one shared transformer architecture encoder on concatenated multilingual corpora using a shared subword vocabulary plus masked language modeling and next-sentence prediction, with language-balanced sampling to encourage cross-lingual parameter sharing.
[5] Mech: It introduces a neural reader that uses recurrent sequence encoders for both document and query and predicts an answer token or span by conditioning the document representation on the query in an end-to-end supervised model trained from raw text.
[6] Mech: Model-B pre-trains a deep transformer architecture encoder with fully bidirectional self-attention mechanism using masked language modeling and next sentence prediction over inputs represented by summed token, segment, and position embeddings, then fine-tunes the same network with minimal task-specific heads.
[7] Mech: It proposes an end-to-end machine reading system that combines statistical entity recognition, relation extraction, coreference resolution, and confidence-aware evidence aggregation to populate a growing knowledge base from web-scale corpora.
[8] Mech: XLM pretrains a single shared transformer architecture encoder over multilingual text using masked language modeling plus translation-aware objectives on parallel sentences, with shared subword vocabulary, shared parameters, and language embeddings to align token and sentence representations across languages.
